leafs.detail: Missed playoffs · 5th in 2026 Draft Lottery order

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Leafs
lotl.body: The curtain crashed down on another Toronto Maple Leafs season that promised fireworks but delivered duds - a 32-36-14 thud landing them 8th in the Atlantic, 35 games back, with a measly 78 points and an L7 streak to salt the wound. No playoffs, no excuses; this core's grip on the Cup slipped further, exposed by defensive sieves and goaltending that crumbled under pressure, finishing 15th in the playoff seed race. Captain Auston Matthews carried the load as always, his sniper's touch the lone bright spot in a lineup that leaked goals like a sieve - yet even he couldn't drag them past mediocrity. Whispers of coaching upheaval swirl around Craig Berube, whose systems never gelled, while free agency looms with UFAs like Mitch Marner eyeing the door and draft positioning offering a sliver of hope at a high lottery pick. The suits in the Air Canada Centre face a reckoning: rebuild or retool around the Big Three? It's gut-check time - no more half-measures. Watch the draft lottery draw on May 6; it could redefine this franchise's next decade.

the_latest.headline: Report: Leafs to interview Mats Sundin for hockey operations role
the_latest.dek: The Hall of Famer has met with Keith Pelley about an advisory position, per Friedman.

the_latest.headline: Will the Maple Leafs rise or regress next season as expectations grow?
the_latest.dek: Sportsnet panel weighs whether the core can bounce back, or whether a harder reset is coming after the first-round exit.

the_latest.headline: Maple Leafs cleanout day: full media availability
the_latest.dek: Auston Matthews, John Tavares, William Nylander and coach Craig Berube address an abrupt end to the season.

the_latest.headline: NHL sets date for 2026 Draft Lottery
the_latest.dek: The lottery runs Tuesday, May 5 on Sportsnet from the NHL Network studio in Secaucus, N.J.

draft_board.label: NHL Draft Lottery
draft_board.notes: Top-5 protected pick owed to Boston from the Brandon Carlo trade. Toronto keeps it if no team from 6th-or-worse jumps into the top 5.

live_strip.text: Today 4:10 PM ET at Arizona · Sportsnet

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Jays
lotl.body: The Blue Jays' early-season dreams are curdling into a sour mess - three straight losses, bats silenced, and the AL East fading in the rearview. Last night's 3-6 defeat to the Diamondbacks in Phoenix rubbed salt in the wound, capping a brutal skid after that lone 7-12 thriller over the Brewers four days back. Sitting at 7-12, fifth in the division and 4 games behind, they're hemorrhaging momentum - only 2.5 runs above the basement by my count. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. stranded in key spots again, his step late on a Diamondback heater that ended the eighth; the lineup's gone quiet since Ernie Clement's .288 swing offered faint hope. No fresh injuries screaming from the trainer's room, but the bullpen's betrayal - leaking three in the middle frames - stings deepest. This isn't contender fire; it's a wake-up call before the roster unravels. Tonight's rubber match at Arizona, 7:10 ET, demands a spark - or the hole digs deeper.

the_latest.headline: Diamondbacks 6, Blue Jays 3: game recap and box score
the_latest.dek: Toronto stranded 10 runners as Arizona broke the game open in the sixth. Myles Straw had a solo shot in the loss.

the_latest.headline: Varsho exits Blue Jays' loss with left knee discomfort
the_latest.dek: Day-to-day, no imaging planned. Straw replaced him in centre after the third inning.

the_latest.headline: Jays call up Eloy Jim√©nez as Springer heads to IL
the_latest.dek: Springer went on the 10-day IL with a fractured left big toe; Jim√©nez joins the active roster in his spot.

the_latest.headline: Blue Jays' offensive slide continues in sloppy loss to Diamondbacks
the_latest.dek: Three straight losses and 17 runs across six games; the Jays slip to 7-12 heading into the Angels series.

live_strip.text: Trail 0-1 · Game 2 Mon 4/20 at Cleveland, 7:00 PM ET, Peacock/NBCSN

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Raptors
lotl.body: The air crackles in Toronto this morning - the Raptors, back in the playoffs for the first time since 2022, stand on the brink after a 46-36 grind that locked the 5th seed and 3rd in the Atlantic. Immanuel Quickley, their sharpshooting sparkplug averaging 16.4 points and a career-high 5.9 assists, shook off hamstring tightness Friday, declaring he'll suit up despite the questionable tag - his second-half three-point surge to 41.5 percent could torch Cleveland's defense. Scottie Barnes looms as the x-factor, with Kevin Garnett crowning him a "Mini Joker" ready to bully the paint, while whispers swirl around Jakob Poeltl's injury status. The regular season's ghosts - that final Nets exit - fade against this electric reset. All eyes lock on Quicken Loans Arena, where the Cavs lurk as heavy favorites. Game 1 tips at 1 p.m. ET today - win here, and the North awakens; falter, and the dream slips. This is why they rebuilt.

the_latest.headline: Cavaliers 126, Raptors 113: Mitchell pours in 32 in Game 1
the_latest.dek: Cleveland led by 24 in the fourth; Barrett had 24 and Shead (starting for injured Quickley) added 17 with five triples.

the_latest.headline: Raptors' Quickley confident he'll be available vs. Cavaliers
the_latest.dek: Toronto's starting PG said Friday he expects to play despite the hamstring strain he aggravated in the regular-season finale.

the_latest.headline: Jamal Shead on starting Game 1: playoff practice, April 17
the_latest.dek: Shead on his mindset entering the postseason and sharing ball-handling reps with Quickley.

the_latest.headline: The definitive Raptors vs Cavs playoff preview
the_latest.dek: Matchup breakdown of Mitchell-Mobley vs Barnes-IQ, bench depth, and the half-court offence keys.

commanders.detail: 3rd NFC East · Pick #7 in 2026 NFL Draft

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Commanders
lotl.body: The draft room hums with that familiar tension in D.C., where a 5-12 slog to third in the NFC East - nine games back, mind you - hands Adam Peters the No. 7 pick like a loaded pistol aimed at redemption. Just two days ago, Peters doubled down: "more likely than not" they stay put, no fire sale trade back unless the board falls just right - though whispers of NFC East rivals like the Cowboys itching to leapfrog add that delicious divisional spice. Mock drafts swarm around Jayden Daniels' arsenal, with Bleacher Report's scouts tabbing Ohio State's Carnell Tate as the WR1 prize to unleash opposite Terry McLaurin, while others eye Notre Dame's Jeremiyah Love or Alabama's Sonny Styles to fortify Dan Quinn's injury-ravaged defense. Free agency's quiet splash - bolstering the line with holdovers like Josh Conerly - screams urgency for skill bombs and secondary steel at 7, 71, and beyond. Peters covets "impact" traits, hosting wideouts like Tate and USC's Makai Lemon, all to armor Daniels after last year's NFC Championship heartbreak. Five days out, Pittsburgh awaits - watch if Peters flips the script for extra ammo or pulls the trigger on a game-changer Thursday night.

the_latest.headline: Commanders 2026 NFL Draft picks, needs and analysis
the_latest.dek: Six selections starting with No. 7 overall. WR, CB and C top the shopping list around Jayden Daniels.

the_latest.headline: Mock Draft Madness 8.0: final first-round projections
the_latest.dek: Washington's in-house board, with WR Carnell Tate emerging as the consensus pick at No. 7.

the_latest.headline: 2026 NFL Draft order: every pick, Rounds 1-7
the_latest.dek: The complete draft board. Commanders pick #7, #71, #147, #187, #209 and #223.

the_latest.headline: Behold the Burgundy and Gold: new uniform reveal
the_latest.dek: Washington's new jersey set leans into the throwback palette, with modern black accents on the striping.

draft_board.label: 2026 NFL Draft
draft_board.notes: Round 1 (#7), Round 3 (#71), Round 5 (#147), Round 6 (#187 and #209), Round 7 (#223). Needs: WR, CB, C. Mock-draft consensus at No. 7: WR Carnell Tate (Ohio State).

featured.headline: Cavaliers roll Raptors 126-113 in Game 1 behind Mitchell and Strus
featured.dek: Donovan Mitchell poured in 32 and Max Strus added 24 off the bench as Cleveland pulled away on a 27-9 run spanning the second and third quarters. RJ Barrett led Toronto with 24 in its first playoff game since 2022.

two_up.headline: Varsho exits Blue Jays' loss with left knee discomfort
two_up.dek: The centre fielder came out in the third after aggravating pre-existing quad soreness. Manager John Schneider said he's day-to-day and won't need imaging.

two_up.headline: Report: Leafs interviewing Mats Sundin for hockey operations role
two_up.dek: The franchise's all-time leading scorer has met with MLSE president Keith Pelley about an advisory position, per Friedman, as Toronto retools after a first-round exit.

extra_story.headline: Commanders' 2026 NFL Draft picks, needs and analysis
extra_story.dek: Washington holds the No. 7 overall pick and five more selections starting Thursday. ESPN breaks down the board, the needs (WR, CB, C), and the likely targets for Jayden Daniels' supporting cast.
